title: Add Friends Chat Bar with Friend List and 1000 Friend Limit

requirements.text: In FriendsModePage.tsx, add a persistent chat bar UI element positioned below the user's profile section. The bar must display a 'Friends' label or chat icon and be tappable/clickable to open a friends panel or drawer.

quotes: Add a chat bar below the profile and once its pressed

acceptanceCriteria: A chat/friends bar is visible below the user profile section on FriendsModePage.tsx
Tapping or clicking the bar opens a friends panel or slide-up drawer
The bar is styled consistently with the Sonic visual theme (cobalt blue/yellow, Fredoka One or Nunito font, rounded styling)

requirements.text: When the friends panel is opened and the user has no friends, display a clear 'No Friends' empty state message with a friendly prompt encouraging them to add friends.

quotes: show no friends when you have no friends

acceptanceCriteria: When the authenticated user has zero accepted friends, the panel shows a 'No Friends' empty state message
The empty state is styled consistently with the Sonic visual theme
No friend list items are rendered when the friend count is zero

requirements.text: When the user has one or more friends, display the friend list in the friends panel. Enforce a maximum friend limit of 1000 — if the user already has 1000 friends, prevent adding any more and show a 'Friend limit reached (1000/1000)' message. Store the friend relationships in the backend, associated with each user's principal.

quotes: when you have friends the friends max limit is 1000

acceptanceCriteria: The friends panel lists all accepted friends when the user has at least one friend
The total number of displayed friends cannot exceed 1000
When the friend count reaches 1000, the 'Add Friend' action is disabled and a 'Friend limit reached (1000/1000)' notice is shown
Friend relationships are persisted in the backend keyed by the user's principal
The backend enforces the 1000-friend cap and returns an error if exceeded

requirements.text: Add a backend data model and functions for friend relationships: a FriendRequest record type with fields requesterId (Text), recipientId (Text), status (Text — 'pending', 'accepted', 'declined'), and sentAt (Int timestamp). Expose the following functions: sendFriendRequest(recipientPrincipal: Text) — creates a pending request; respondToFriendRequest(requesterId: Text, accept: Bool) — accepts or declines a request; getFriends() — returns all accepted friends of the calling principal; getFriendRequests() — returns all incoming pending requests for the calling principal. Enforce the 1000-friend cap in sendFriendRequest.

quotes: add a add as a trusted friend and they will press accept trusted friend request

acceptanceCriteria: A stable FriendRequest record array is stored in backend/main.mo
sendFriendRequest creates a new pending request and returns an error if the caller already has 1000 accepted friends
respondToFriendRequest updates the request status to 'accepted' or 'declined'
getFriends returns only accepted friend records for the calling principal
getFriendRequests returns all pending incoming requests for the calling principal

requirements.text: In the friends panel on FriendsModePage.tsx, display incoming pending friend requests as a separate section labelled 'Friend Requests'. Each pending request must show the requester's display name and two action buttons: 'Accept' (calls respondToFriendRequest with accept=true) and 'Decline' (calls respondToFriendRequest with accept=false). After accepting, the new friend appears in the friends list immediately.

quotes: add a add as a trusted friend and they will press accept trusted friend request on the person name

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Friend Requests' section is shown in the friends panel when there are pending incoming requests
Each pending request shows the requester's display name
Accept and Decline buttons are present for each request
Accepting a request moves the user into the friends list without a full page reload
Declining a request removes it from the pending list

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture)
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Friend relationships and request state must be persisted in the backend, not only in localStorage
The 1000-friend cap must be enforced on both frontend and backend
nonGoals: Real-time chat messaging between friends (WebSockets or push notifications are not supported)
Friend search by name/display name (not included in this request)
Age-matched peer chat (separate from friend list, not in scope for this request)
